cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise to Leadership Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hicks transitions from a capable marine to the de facto leader of the surviving team after Gorman's incapacitation, asserting command during critical moments of retreat and crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Recognized leader maintaining group cohesion under extreme duress
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Formal chain of command remains ambiguous; leadership is situational and unconfirmed

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Combat to Protection: The Newt Rescue Arc
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hicks shifts from a mission focused on combat and evacuation to one centered on protecting and rescuing Newt, culminating in his prioritization of survival over emotional attachment when she is lost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Focused on locating colonists and minimizing casualties
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Accepts Newt's apparent death and enforces survival-focused retreat
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Newt's fate remains unknown, leaving emotional and moral ambiguity

cross_scene_arcs.title: Hicks and Ripley: Forging a Survival Partnership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hicks and Ripley develop a deepening bond of trust and mutual reliance, evolving from professional cooperation to emotional intimacy as they become the core of the surviving team.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Initial rapport based on shared threat assessment
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emotional connection solidified through shared leadership and vulnerability
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Their relationship is cut short by Hicks' incapacitation, with no closure on its future

cross_scene_arcs.title: Collapse of Escape and Adaptation to Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The destruction of the drop-ship forces Hicks to abandon evacuation plans and lead the team into a desperate defensive stand, confronting the conflict between dwindling resources and overwhelming alien threat.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Team committed to ground-based defense with no escape in sight
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Defensive plan is fragile and may not withstand sustained xenomorph assault

cross_scene_arcs.title: Command Under Siege: Holding the Line
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hicks repeatedly demonstrates decisive command during active combat, making life-or-death decisions to secure the APC, repel breaches, and maintain team survival amid cascading failures.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Proven combat leader who preserves the group through tactical and physical intervention
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Team is whittled down to a few survivors, and Hicks' own injuries threaten his continued effectiveness

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Trust: The Bond with Ripley
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Newt evolves from a feral, emotionally shut-down survivor into a child capable of forming a deep maternal bond with Ripley, transitioning from distrust and silence to calling her 'Mommy' in a moment of emotional catharsis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Psychologically shattered, nonverbal, and resistant to human contact after years of solitary survival.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emotionally anchored and safe, having formed a surrogate mother-daughter bond with Ripley.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While the bond is solidified, long-term emotional healing from trauma remains an ongoing process.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Struggle for Survival and Agency
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Newt progresses from passive survival in isolation to active participation in escape, only to be repeatedly captured and endangered, culminating in her ultimate rescue and evacuation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Goal is mere concealment and avoidance; survival through invisibility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Physically safe aboard the drop-ship, having transitioned from victim to participant and finally to survivor.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her physical safety is secured, but the psychological toll of repeated near-death experiences remains unhealed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trauma, Loss, and the Search for Safety
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Newt's internal conflict centers on overcoming paralyzing trauma from the loss of her family and the horrors she has endured, gradually finding moments of comfort and safety through Ripley's care, yet remaining haunted by nightmares and fear.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Overwhelmed by hysteria and fear after witnessing her father's attack and hearing her mother's mayday.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Able to rest and emotionally release after prolonged trauma, now that immediate danger has passed.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Nightmares and deep psychological wounds persist, indicating that recovery will require long-term support.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Shifting Maternal Attachment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Newt's emotional dependency shifts from her biological mother to Ripley, symbolizing her psychological adaptation to loss and her need for a new caregiver in the aftermath of familial annihilation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Calling out for her mother in distress, clinging to the memory of her lost family.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Calling Ripley 'Mommy,' signifying full emotional transfer of maternal attachment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The permanence of this bond is untested beyond the crisis, and Newt's fear of future abandonment lingers beneath the surface.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Endurance Under Extreme Peril
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Newt's status fluctuates between rescued, captured, hunted, and saved, reflecting her precarious position as both a target and a symbol of vulnerability in the alien threat hierarchy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Found alive and beginning rescue, transitioning from isolation to connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Survives decompression and is secured, having endured the most extreme physical dangers aboard the station.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her survival hinges entirely on others; her autonomy remains limited by ongoing external threats until the final moments.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Shattered Promise: Grief and Loss Across Time
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley's hope of reuniting with her daughter Amanda is destroyed when she learns of her death after 57 years of time dilation, forcing her to confront irreversible loss and the emotional chasm created by decades of absence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley clings to the hope of fulfilling her promise to return home and reconnect with her daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is emotionally devastated, isolated, and must accept that she can never make amends or reclaim lost time.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How to carry forward with her life and mission in the face of profound personal grief and irreversible loss.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Discredited Survivor to Reluctant Warrior
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley transitions from being publicly discredited and dismissed to reclaiming authority as the sole expert on the xenomorph threat, ultimately becoming a central figure in the mission despite institutional betrayal.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley is rejected by authorities, stripped of credibility, and dismissed as unreliable.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She assumes authoritative control by briefing the marines and validating her expertise through critical observations.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The marines' skepticism and Burke's potential betrayal continue to undermine her hard-won authority.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Birth of a Maternal Bond: From Duty to Sacrifice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley evolves from a mission-focused survivor into a protective maternal figure after discovering Newt, culminating in a deep, reciprocal bond that becomes the emotional core of her motivation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley begins searching for survivors with no expectation of emotional connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She fully embraces Newt as her child, finding emotional release and fulfillment in their bond after surviving together.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The stability of their bond is threatened by ongoing trauma and the uncertainty of survival in a hostile world.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Hive: Strategy, Betrayal, and the Queen
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley shifts from confirming the xenomorph presence to formulating a strategic response, identifying the Queen as the key threat, and ultimately engaging in a final battle to destroy her and ensure Newt's safety.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley suspects xenomorph activity and seeks confirmation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She actively destroys the Queen's egg sac and defeats the Queen in combat, eliminating the immediate threat.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The psychological toll of constant combat and the possibility of future encounters with the species remain unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Clarity Amid Corporate Corruption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley faces escalating conflict with Burke, whose corporate agenda endangers the mission; she rejects complicity, exposes his betrayal, and fights to uphold ethical survival over profit-driven exploitation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley demands assurance that the mission's goal is extermination, not exploitation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She violently rejects Burke's offer, survives his betrayal, and ensures the mission remains focused on survival and protection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Burke's influence and the larger corporate conspiracy suggest systemic dangers that outlive his personal defeat.
